title: update build.py as to use env prioritized
body: The PR requests to adjust the build process so that local developers can build CursorPro using their own environment settings. Specifically, when packaging, the build should prefer a local .env file if it exists (copy it into the build output as .env); if not, fall back to using .env.example. Previously, the script always copied .env.example to the output. This change enables local, env-driven builds without requiring changes to the repository, while preserving a fallback to env.example for new developers.

step_plan.summary: This PR updates build.py to prefer a local .env during packaging, falling back to .env.example if none is present. This enables env-driven local builds while preserving existing config.ini handling.

steps.operation: Modify env copy logic in build() to prioritize local .env over .env.example during packaging
steps.reason: To allow local environment settings to drive the build output by embedding a local .env when present; if not, fall back to copying .env.example. This preserves existing config.ini behavior and enables env-driven local builds without requiring repository changes.
steps.side_effects: Potential exposure of secrets if a real .env is copied into the dist; add safeguards or opt-in gating (e.g., BUILD_USE_LOCAL_ENV) to prevent accidental leakage. Tests should cover three scenarios: (1) .env present -> dist contains .env; (2) only .env.example present -> dist contains .env copied from .env.example; (3) neither present -> env copy is skipped. Downstream packaging and CI workflows may need updates to reflect the new behavior and to ensure dist artifacts are treated appropriately.
